item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An article that somehow manages to roll a creepy atmosphere together with making the reader feel sympathy for the anomaly.
+1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Turns out we actually have quite a few SCP articles in which fetuses are replaced with things that are not fetuses, including SCP-886, SCP-1263, and SCP-1586. It would not be too far out of line for Johnny Foundation to hypothesize that 1486 may tap into a variant of the phenomena used in any of these other anomalies to achieve its effect. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

If a fertile, human couple of opposite sexes completes copulation within ~50 meters of SCP-1486 (regardless of contraceptive use), SCP-1486 will disappear from where it has been placed and appear in the female subject's uterus at less than 0.01% of its original size. SCP-1486 will then begin slowly increasing in size at the rate of a normal embryo and foetus1. Ultrasound testing of the subject has shown that SCP-1486 retains its shape throughout the growth process. Subjects may sustain damage to the uterine wall, causing infertility. Excretions from SCP-1486 will exit through the cervix.

To this:

If a fertile, human couple of opposite sexes completes copulation within ~50 meters of SCP-1486 (regardless of contraceptive use), SCP-1486 will disappear from where it has been placed and appear in the female subject's uterus at less than 0.01% of its original size2. SCP-1486 will then begin slowly increasing in size at the rate of a normal embryo and foetus3. Ultrasound testing of the subject has shown that SCP-1486 retains its shape throughout the growth process. Subjects may sustain damage to the uterine wall, causing infertility. Excretions from SCP-1486 will exit through the cervix.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. For financial and ethical concerns, testing regarding the subject body's acceptance and nourishment of SCP-1486 has not been performed. For more information read Dr. Fulnan's paper: SCP-1486: Hypotheses regarding the placenta and immune system.
2. Whether this effect is achieved through use of the same phenomena as observed in SCP-886, SCP-1263, SCP-1586, or a combination thereof is currently unknown.
3. For financial and ethical concerns, testing regarding the subject body's acceptance and nourishment of SCP-1486 has not been performed. For more information read Dr. Fulnan's paper: SCP-1486: Hypotheses regarding the placenta and immune system.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff Notice: this user has been permanently banned for trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should have been horrifying and ended up being pretty sad, who would have thought?

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow reminded me of chucky for some reason…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking brilliant. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I want to pity it, if I birthed that monstrosity I would throw it away, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the perfect combination of scary and sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: can we at least give him cigarettes and human interaction as part of containment maaaaaaan :<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^ Ditto. Though maybe not the hug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was ready top hate this thing. Creepy photo, strange baby doll growing and moving on its own. Then I read the interview. Then I got to the end of the interview. Now I just want to clean him off and hug him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of hate you now. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"(regardless of contraceptive use), SCP-1486 will disappear from where it has been placed and appear in the female subject's uterus"

What if she's already pregnant? Or what if she's had a hysterectomy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nailed it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"hammer it in"…
My squick! Oh, God! It's everywhere!

Those three words imply that someone did that to Benny. And he remembers it, even if he refuses to conciously acknowledge the facts. And the physical trauma is nothing to him, compared to the pain of abandonment and mother-rejection. I have never read a clearer or more realistic depiction of emotional trauma and coping mechanisms in my life.

The fact that you did it with a mangled kewpie doll is just showing off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cropped the picture a bit, but when I'd found it there was more of another chair and, IIRC, a guy's leg from somebody sitting in said chair. So, presumably some guys were just goofing around making creepy art and then taking pictures. It happens more frequently then you might think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't want to like this, but the interview is just too perfect.

Also you have to wonder how they can really enforce that "no sex" perimeter. They should refer to it as the Champange Room.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw picture… immediately clicked "+1". Seriously though, what the hell is that thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel sick, and sad, and mostly sick.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well the mainlist title is "Benny", so it's definitely Mordechai.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely gorgeous and horrifying. So what's the little tyke's name, Oedipus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Articles like this that give me a slew of conflicting emotions tend to be the best.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, well, uh, obviously!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: RU-486

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Horrifying yet touching in a very strange way. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture is horrifying. The description is horrifying. The conversation is horrifying. Everything about this is horrifying.

I'll give this an upvote but don't expect me at your birthday party.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DAMMIT sal, I didn't want to upvote this but then the last quarter of so of that log… just take the upvote and make it go away!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um… yeah, of course. But, uh, just to make sure I'm not giving away any big secrets, why don't you, uh, tell me what kind of connection this SCP would have with its designation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you deliberately pick the number "1486" for this one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…come here sweetie, let me hug you. :c

(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +!, but I actually liked it better before I read the logs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is exceedingly gross, but I find it adorable.
Why do I find this thing adorable?
+1 Confliction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
H-how did you make this so gross yet also so- eurgh. Touching? I DON'T WANT TO FEEL FOR THE METAL PUS-BABY BUT I DO.

+1, you monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delicious! (Yeah, the word I got to describe it was delicious). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel sorry for the terrifying doll thing. They would be possibly the easiest to enforce scp procedures ever assuming all site staff have read the interview log *shudder*.

+1

Edit: me no type good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, Who Framed Roger Rabbit was strong for me, too. But this definitely took it in a much different direction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excretions from SCP-1468 will exit through the cervix.

Once it has been birthed, SCP-1486 will search for the subject mother and in some instances, SCP-1486 will attempt to breastfeed unless restrained.

My insides feel vaguely uneasy, and it's all thanks to you.
Yep, you squicked out the guy who writes about magical corpsefuckers.
+1 and I'm thoroughly impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the darkly humorous tone on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It felt like it was trying to cope through humor. Which is what secured my upvasdgas;dfa jesus my mom is making dinner and it smells delicious, I can't focus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
funny articles

"Well, yeah, but I mean they hate me. How could a mother hate her own kid, doc? Enough to hurt them so much?"

uwotm8

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Salman, you are the undisputed master of making funny articles that don't suck.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda expected this to act like a humorous, womanizing baby that speaks gruffly in a Boston accent.

Then I read the interview.

Oh, lord, just… take it. Take the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the best present ever

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That means Larry is also your birthday SCP. Congrats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How could they just hammer it in like that?

The picture has nails it. Lovely.

Also, re-reading it, I started thinking of it less as a doll, and more as an indestructible, but damageable, child. Which is another lovely thought. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: omg sal we have the same birthday

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This year my birthday is landing on Mother's Day, so I figured why not do an SCP about mothers and birth? Those are two very joyful things! Right?

…Right?

Thanks to Wilt for the image, and Moose, Showshoe, Flameshirt, and Vivax for some expert advice.
